simulations.description: Grid of features each with icon, title, short description. Common alternative to bento.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Zero Latency

Sub-20ms inference on any model.

...
...
...
...
...
naive_failures: feature-icon div (contains inline SVG) → classified as HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX (too aggressive — SVG is purely decorative icon)
SVG inside icon div → triggers HTML_WIDGET on entire feature-item
Result: all 6 cards become HTML widgets, titles and descriptions lose editability

corrected_output.feature-icon: HTML_WIDGET — icon container with inline SVG (editability 2/10, designer-maintained)
corrected_output.key_rule: Inline SVG that is PURELY decorative (no interactive children, no text, no click handler) = HTML_WIDGET for the icon container only. Parent card and text siblings stay native.

delta.root_cause: SVG detection triggers HTML_WIDGET on entire parent card, not just the SVG container
delta.fix: SVG isolation rule: if SVG is inside a small dedicated icon container (width/height <= 64px), classify ONLY that container as HTML_WIDGET. Do not propagate to card parent.
delta.new_rule: SVG_ISOLATION: small icon container with inline SVG → HTML_WIDGET for icon only, siblings remain native

svg_isolation_rule.trigger: inline SVG inside container with width AND height both <= 64px
svg_isolation_rule.action: Make ONLY that container HTML_WIDGET. Do not affect parent or siblings.

simulations.description: Classic feature section: image left + text right, then text left + image right, alternating.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
VISUAL BUILDER

Build pipelines without code

Drag, connect, and deploy...

SEE HOW IT WORKS
ANALYTICS

Full visibility into every run

...
naive_failures: flex-direction:row-reverse on reversed row → not supported in Elementor flex settings
Naive engine ignores row-reverse modifier → both rows render same direction
feature-row__image with img → IMAGE_WIDGET (correct)
feature-row__tag → TEXT_WIDGET (should be HTML_WIDGET for ::before line)

corrected_output.reversed_row: Elementor has no flex-direction:row-reverse. Solution: swap child order in JSON (image container first in DOM, content second) + note in report.
corrected_output.image_col: FLEX_COLUMN inner container (50% width) containing IMAGE_WIDGET
corrected_output.content_col: FLEX_COLUMN inner container (flex:1) with tag + heading + text + button
corrected_output.companion_css_note: Add nx-feature-row--reversed class. Companion CSS: .nx-feature-row--reversed { flex-direction: row-reverse; } — Elementor panel shows 'row' but CSS overrides to row-reverse at runtime

delta.root_cause: flex-direction:row-reverse has no Elementor panel equivalent
delta.fix_option_1: Reorder children in JSON (image first, text second) for reversed rows — visual result is the same as row-reverse when both children take 50%
delta.fix_option_2: Apply row-reverse via companion CSS targeting the reversed row's class
delta.recommended_fix: Option 1 (reorder children) for full-width equal columns. Option 2 (companion CSS) when widths are unequal.
delta.new_rule: FLEX_ROW_REVERSE: detect flex-direction:row-reverse on container. If children are equal width → reorder in JSON. If unequal → companion CSS override.

row_reverse_rule.action_unequal_width: Keep order, add flex-direction:row-reverse to companion CSS

simulations.description: Vertical or horizontal timeline showing company history, roadmap, or process milestones.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Q1 2024

Platform Launch

Launched with 10 integrations and 500 beta users.

...
naive_failures: timeline-line (position:absolute decorative line) → classified as DECORATIVE and skipped — correct!
calc(50% + 40px) padding on items → not expressible in Elementor panel (fixed px only)
timeline-item--left/right alternating structure → Elementor has no built-in timeline widget
Result: structurally complex layout forced into nested containers that break the visual

corrected_output.timeline-line: SKIP from JSON — recreate as .nx-timeline-track::before in companion CSS
corrected_output.each_timeline_item: FLEX_ROW inner container (class: nx-timeline-item, nx-timeline-item--left OR nx-timeline-item--right)
corrected_output.timeline-card: FLEX_COLUMN inner container — holds date (TEXT_EDITOR), title (HEADING h4), desc (TEXT_EDITOR)
corrected_output.calc_padding: Cannot set calc() in Elementor padding. Use companion CSS: .nx-timeline-item--left { justify-content: flex-end; padding-right: calc(50% + 40px); }
corrected_output.companion_css_central_line: .nx-timeline-track { position: relative; } .nx-timeline-track::before { content:''; position:absolute; left:50%; top:0; bottom:0; width:1px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.1); transform:translateX(-50%); }
corrected_output.editability: Timeline dates, titles, descriptions → all native and fully editable. Layout positioning → companion CSS.

delta.root_cause_1: calc() values in padding/margin not settable in Elementor — needs companion CSS
delta.root_cause_2: Decorative absolute line (::before equivalent) needs pseudo-element in companion CSS
new_rules: CALC_PADDING: padding/margin with calc() → companion CSS only, set closest px value in Elementor settings as fallback
DECORATIVE_LINE: position:absolute element with no content, zero width or height → recreate as parent::before in companion CSS

calc_padding_rule.detection: padding or margin value contains calc()
calc_padding_rule.action: Move full calc() expression to companion CSS. Use the largest fixed px value from the calc() as Elementor fallback.
calc_padding_rule.example: calc(50% + 40px) → Elementor: 0px (structural spacing handled by companion CSS)

decorative_absolute_line_rule.detection: position:absolute + (width:1px OR height:1px) + no text content + no children
decorative_absolute_line_rule.action: SKIP from JSON, recreate as parent::before pseudo-element in companion CSS

simulations.description: Tab navigation that shows/hides content panels. Common for feature showcases.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Visual Pipeline Builder

...

...
...
naive_failures: display:none on inactive panels → SKIP rule removes them entirely from JSON
Tab buttons with JS click handlers → HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED (overly aggressive for simple class toggles)
Result: only the active tab panel is converted, all other content lost

reason: Tab show/hide is JS-driven — inseparable from the toggle script
display:none panels must NOT be skipped — they contain real content
The JS is simple enough to include inline in HTML widget
corrected_output.editability_note: Tab component is HTML_WIDGET. Label text and content is in the widget HTML source. Editability: 5/10 — a developer can update content in the widget, not a click-and-type experience.
corrected_output.content_extraction_note: Extract all tab panel content into the HTML widget with clear HTML comments: <!-- TAB: PIPELINES --> so editor knows where to find each tab's content

delta.root_cause: display:none detection rule skips hidden elements — correct for static hidden elements, wrong for JS-toggled tab panels
delta.fix: SKIP rule exception: if hidden element has a sibling JS script containing querySelector + style.display toggle referencing this element's ID → do NOT skip, include in HTML_WIDGET

display_none_rule_refinement.skip_if: display:none AND no JS references to this element's ID/class AND no data-tab/data-panel attribute
display_none_rule_refinement.include_if: display:none AND JS toggles its display OR has data-tab/data-panel attribute (tab system)

display_none_exception_rule.rule: display:none siblings where one sibling is visible and others are hidden with same class pattern → TAB_SYSTEM, include all in HTML_WIDGET
display_none_exception_rule.additional_trigger: data-tab, data-panel, data-target attributes on nav elements

simulations.description: Expandable question/answer list. Very common in pricing and help sections.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
NEXUS analyses each task...

...
naive_failures: faq-answer with display:none → SKIP (wrong — same tab panel problem)
faq-question <button> → BUTTON_WIDGET (wrong — it's an accordion trigger, not a CTA)
faq-icon span (+/-) → DECORATIVE (correct)

corrected_output.elementor_native_alternative: Elementor Pro has an Accordion widget. If Pro is detected, suggest using it instead. For Free: HTML_WIDGET.
corrected_output.pro_detection: Check if Elementor Pro active via WP plugin check during conversion
corrected_output.content_structure_in_widget: Each faq-item has clear HTML structure — questions and answers are easily editable in widget source
corrected_output.editability: 6/10 — questions and answers in HTML widget source, easy to find and update

delta.root_cause: display:none on faq-answer triggers SKIP — same issue as tabs
delta.fix: Extend display:none exception: if hidden element is sibling of a <button> with click handler → ACCORDION_SYSTEM, include in HTML_WIDGET
delta.pro_recommendation: When Elementor Pro is active, offer Accordion widget as alternative to HTML_WIDGET for FAQ patterns

simulations.description: Hero or section split exactly 50/50 where one half is a solid colour and the other is content or image.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
AI PLATFORM

Build
faster.

GET STARTED →
naive_failures: grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr → GRID_CONTAINER (correct)
split-left with padding:80px → correct as FLEX_COLUMN inner container
split-tag div → TEXT_WIDGET (should be HTML_WIDGET for ::before decoration OR just TEXT_EDITOR with class)
split-cta align-self:flex-start → not settable in Elementor button widget settings → companion CSS needed
split-right as image column → correct as FLEX_COLUMN with IMAGE_WIDGET

corrected_output.split_left: FLEX_COLUMN inner container — bg:#c8ff00, padding:80px, justify:center
corrected_output.split_tag: TEXT_EDITOR (with class nx-split-tag — companion CSS handles ::before decoration if needed)
corrected_output.split_right: FLEX_COLUMN inner container — bg:#0a0a12, align:center, justify:center
corrected_output.align_self_rule: align-self on a button or widget → companion CSS targeting .elementor-widget-[type] or .elementor-widget-container

delta.root_cause: align-self property on individual child not settable in Elementor
delta.fix: ALIGN_SELF companion CSS rule: detect align-self on any element → companion CSS targeting the widget's elementor-widget-container wrapper
delta.new_rule: ALIGN_SELF: element has align-self → companion CSS: .nx-[class] .elementor-widget, .nx-[class].elementor-widget { align-self: [value]; }

align_self_rule.detection: align-self: flex-start | center | flex-end | stretch on any element

simulations.description: Process steps without a visual panel — just the numbered list taking full width or 2-column grid.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
01

Connect

Link your data sources in minutes.

02

Configure

...
naive_failures: step-number with large font (48px) + low opacity → correctly classified as TEXT_WIDGET but editability wrongly set to 8/10 (it's actually 3/10 — clients rarely change step numbers)
2-col grid of steps → GRID_CONTAINER correct
Overall classification: mostly correct, editability scoring wrong

corrected_output.companion_css: .nx-step-number .elementor-widget-text-editor p { font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 48px; font-weight: 700; color: rgba(200,255,0,.2); line-height: 1; }

delta.root_cause: Large decorative numbers (01, 02, 03) get high editability score because they are headings — but they are ordinal markers, not client-editable copy
delta.fix: ORDINAL_MARKER detection: element contains only 1-2 digit number + font-size >= 40px + low opacity (< 0.4) → editability: 3/10 (not 8/10)
delta.new_signal: ordinal_marker_fingerprint: number-only text + large font + low opacity = decorative ordinal, low editability

simulations.description: Grid of team member cards with photo, name, title.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Sarah Kim

VP of Engineering
naive_failures: team-socials with SVG links → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX on entire card
team-photo with aspect-ratio:1 → not settable in Elementor image settings
object-fit:cover on img → not settable in IMAGE_WIDGET settings

corrected_output.team-photo: IMAGE_WIDGET (class: nx-team-photo) — companion CSS: .nx-team-photo img { aspect-ratio:1; object-fit:cover; width:100%; border-radius:8px; }
corrected_output.team-socials: HTML_WIDGET — social links with SVG icons (low editability: 2/10, designer-maintained)
corrected_output.key_insight: Social icon links are low-editability decorative components. Make them HTML_WIDGET. The rest of the card is native.

delta.root_cause_1: SVG social links causing entire card to become HTML_WIDGET
delta.root_cause_2: aspect-ratio and object-fit on images not in Elementor settings
delta.fix_1: SVG isolation rule applied — social links become HTML_WIDGET, card stays native (same rule as SIM-067 icon)
delta.fix_2: aspect-ratio + object-fit → companion CSS on IMAGE_WIDGET class
new_css_rules: ASPECT_RATIO: aspect-ratio on image container → companion CSS on nx-[class] img
OBJECT_FIT: object-fit:cover on img → companion CSS on nx-[class] img { object-fit:cover; }

simulations.description: Large styled blockquote or pull quote, often full-width with distinct typography.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
"NEXUS didn't just automate our workflows — it made us rethink how we build products."
— MARCUS OSEI, HEAD OF AI AT CORTEX LABS
naive_failures: blockquote tag → TEXT_WIDGET (correct structure, wrong styling)
border-left:4px solid #c8ff00 → Elementor border settings only support uniform borders, not single-side
cite → TEXT_WIDGET (correct)
Italic large text styling → lost without companion CSS

corrected_output.border_left: Cannot set single-side border in Elementor container settings. Companion CSS: .nx-pull-quote { border-left: 4px solid var(--color-accent); }
corrected_output.blockquote: TEXT_EDITOR (class: nx-pull-quote-text) — companion CSS for italic + large size + letter-spacing
corrected_output.single_side_border_rule: Single-side border (border-left, border-top, border-bottom, border-right only) → companion CSS always

delta.root_cause: Elementor container border settings apply to all sides — cannot set border on one side only via panel
delta.fix: SINGLE_SIDE_BORDER: detect border-left/right/top/bottom (only one side set) → companion CSS only
delta.new_rule: SINGLE_SIDE_BORDER: any border property targeting only one side → companion CSS. Elementor panel border is set to none.

single_side_border_rule.detection: Only one of border-top, border-right, border-bottom, border-left is set (others absent or border:none)
single_side_border_rule.action: Companion CSS only. Set border: none in Elementor settings.

blockquote_tag_rule.action: TEXT_EDITOR widget — blockquote content is client-editable
blockquote_tag_rule.companion_css: Font styling (italic, size, color) → companion CSS targeting .nx-[class] .elementor-widget-text-editor

simulations.description: Comparison table or data table — feature comparison, spec sheet, pricing comparison.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
FEATURE STARTER GROWTH ★ ENTERPRISE
Pipeline count 3 Unlimited Unlimited
naive_failures: <table> → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX (too aggressive for simple data tables)
Table has perfectly editable content (text in cells)
But Elementor Free has no native table widget
Elementor Pro has a Table widget

corrected_output.reason: Elementor Free has no native table widget. Even Elementor Pro's table widget is limited compared to styled HTML tables.

html_widget_enhancement.structure: Keep full table HTML in widget
html_widget_enhancement.companion_css: Table styles go in companion CSS targeting .nx-comparison-table table, th, td
html_widget_enhancement.overflow: Add overflow-x:auto to companion CSS on .nx-comparison-table wrapper for mobile

corrected_output.editability: 7/10 — table content (text in cells) is straightforward to edit in HTML widget source. Rows and columns have clear structure.
corrected_output.report_note: Comparison table preserved as HTML widget. Cell content is editable in the widget source. Each row is a <tr>, each cell is <td>.

delta.root_cause: Table elements have no Elementor native equivalent
delta.fix: TABLE_HARD_RULE: any <table> element → HTML_WIDGET. No confidence calculation needed.

table_hard_rule.action: HTML_WIDGET — hard rule, no confidence cascade
table_hard_rule.priority: Same level as position:fixed hard rule

simulations.description: Row of clickable filter buttons — used for portfolio filters, blog category filters, feature toggles.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
...
...
naive_failures: filter-chip buttons with click handlers → BUTTON_WIDGET (wrong — these are toggle buttons, not CTAs)
filter-content items with display:none → SKIP (wrong — filtered content exists)
JS click handler with display toggle → same problem as tabs

corrected_output.entire_component: HTML_WIDGET — filter bar + filter content + JS are inseparable
corrected_output.reason: JS toggles display:none on content items — same pattern as tabs. Content must not be skipped.
corrected_output.editability: 5/10 — filter labels and content items are in the HTML. Adding/removing filter items requires editing the HTML widget but is straightforward.
corrected_output.content_structure_note: Add HTML comments marking each filter section: <!-- FILTER: AUTOMATION --> for developer clarity

delta.root_cause: data-filter attribute on buttons + display:none on content items = FILTER_SYSTEM, same family as TAB_SYSTEM
delta.fix: Extend display:none exception to cover data-filter, data-category patterns alongside data-tab patterns

simulations.description: Static number showcase — no count-up animation, just large numbers with context.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
4.2B+
API CALLS / MONTH
naive_failures: metric-num with large font → TEXT_EDITOR (correct! No JS here)
Inline <span style='color:#c8ff00'> inside metric-num → TEXT_EDITOR with inline HTML (correct)
No count-up JS detected → classified as native correctly
This is actually a success case — static stats work fine as native

corrected_output.metric-num: TEXT_EDITOR (class: nx-metric-num) — the span with accent colour is preserved in HTML editor content
corrected_output.no_html_widget_needed: Static numbers are fully native and editable

delta.root_cause: None — this pattern classifies correctly already. Simulation confirms: when no JS animation, stats are native.
delta.confirmation: Static stats with no IntersectionObserver, no data-target, no count-up script → ALWAYS native TEXT_EDITOR
delta.distinction_from_SIM016: SIM-016 had count-up JS → HTML_WIDGET. This has no JS → native TEXT_EDITOR. The presence of animation JS is the critical differentiator.

static_vs_animated_stat_rule.rule: Stat display is HTML_WIDGET ONLY when: data-target attribute present OR count-up script present OR IntersectionObserver modifying textContent
static_vs_animated_stat_rule.otherwise: Native TEXT_EDITOR — large numbers are perfectly fine as native widgets

simulations.description: Auto-fill or masonry image/card grid where items have variable heights.
naive_failures: columns:3 → CSS Columns layout — not CSS Grid, not Flexbox. Elementor has no CSS Columns equivalent.
break-inside:avoid → no Elementor equivalent
Naive engine tries to convert to GRID_CONTAINER → breaks masonry layout

corrected_output.reason: CSS Columns layout (not Grid, not Flex) has no Elementor equivalent. The visual masonry effect depends on browser column reflow.
corrected_output.companion_css_approach: Gallery stays HTML_WIDGET. Companion CSS handles column count responsive behaviour.
corrected_output.editability: 4/10 — images are in HTML widget source with clear tags. Swapping images requires updating src attributes.
corrected_output.report_note: Masonry/columns gallery detected. Preserved as HTML widget — CSS Columns layout cannot be replicated in Elementor containers. To update images: edit the HTML widget and update the img src values.

delta.root_cause: CSS Columns layout (columns: N) is not CSS Grid or Flexbox — has no Elementor panel equivalent
delta.fix: CSS_COLUMNS detection: columns: N on a container → HTML_WIDGET. No native equivalent exists.
delta.new_rule: CSS_COLUMNS_HARD_RULE: columns property (CSS Columns layout) → HTML_WIDGET, unconditional

css_columns_hard_rule.trigger: columns: [integer] OR column-count: [integer] CSS property on container
css_columns_hard_rule.action: HTML_WIDGET — hard rule, CSS Columns has no Elementor native equivalent

auto_fill_grid.detection: grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(Npx, 1fr)) OR repeat(auto-fit, minmax(Npx, 1fr))
auto_fill_grid.elementor_handling: Convert to closest fixed column count. auto-fill with minmax(300px, 1fr) in 1200px container ≈ 3 columns. Document in report.

simulations.description: Navigation with dropdown or mega menu panels.
simulations.input_html_pattern:
PRODUCT ▾
⚙
Pipelines Build and deploy AI workflows
PRICING
naive_failures: position:fixed → HTML_WIDGET (correct — this is the nav hard rule)
nav-dropdown with display:none → SKIP (wrong — same tab/accordion problem)
Entire nav is already HTML_WIDGET so dropdown being skipped is less critical — it just won't appear in the template

corrected_output.display_none_exception: Within HTML_WIDGET content, display:none elements are preserved as-is — the JS toggle handles them at runtime
corrected_output.key_insight: When the entire containing section is already HTML_WIDGET, display:none child elements do NOT get SKIP applied. SKIP rule applies only to potential NATIVE widget elements.
corrected_output.scoping_rule: display:none SKIP rule scope = NATIVE widget classification pass only. HTML_WIDGET content is copied verbatim, including hidden elements.

delta.root_cause: display:none SKIP rule applied too broadly — also affected HTML widget internal content
delta.fix: SCOPE the display:none SKIP rule: only apply when classifying elements as potential NATIVE widgets. Once a container is classified as HTML_WIDGET, its descendants are copied as-is.
delta.new_rule: HTML_WIDGET_COPY_VERBATIM: once an element is classified as HTML_WIDGET, copy its entire innerHTML verbatim. Do NOT apply child classification rules to its descendants.

html_widget_verbatim_rule.rule: After classifying a container as HTML_WIDGET, stop descending into its children. Copy innerHTML directly.
html_widget_verbatim_rule.exceptions: Only exception: if the HTML_WIDGET child contains an editable text region flagged by editability score >= 8. In that case, note it in the report as manually extractable.
html_widget_verbatim_rule.rationale: Prevents partial classification of HTML widget internals which produces malformed output

simulations.description: Complex radial/mesh gradient backgrounds — multiple overlapping radial gradients creating a glow effect.
simulations.input_html_pattern: Build workflows
naive_failures: bg-gradient div with complex radial-gradient → companion CSS (correct)
But position:absolute on bg-gradient div → HTML_WIDGET (wrong — too aggressive for a background element)
Content layer (hero-content) gets buried inside HTML_WIDGET when bg-gradient triggers it

corrected_output.bg_gradient: SKIP from JSON — recreate as hero::before in companion CSS
corrected_output.hero_content: Keep as native container with native heading/button widgets
corrected_output.companion_css_gradient: .nx-hero { position: relative; overflow: hidden; } .nx-hero::before { content:''; position:absolute; inset:0; background: radial-gradient(ellipse 80% 60% at 20% 50%, rgba(200,255,0,.12) 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 80% at 80% 20%, rgba(100,200,255,.08) 0%, transparent 50%); pointer-events:none; z-index:0; } .nx-hero > * { position:relative; z-index:2; }
corrected_output.key_insight: Absolutely positioned decorative overlay divs (bg effects, gradient meshes, noise overlays) should become parent::before, not HTML_WIDGET. They have no content.

delta.root_cause: position:absolute on bg-gradient overlay triggering HTML_WIDGET hard rule — but the element has no content, is purely decorative
delta.rule_refinement: REFINE position:fixed hard rule. position:absolute alone is NOT enough to trigger HTML_WIDGET. Must also have interactive content, animation, or JS dependency.
delta.updated_absolute_rule: position:absolute + no text content + no interactive elements + no animation + no JS dependency → DECORATIVE_OVERLAY → companion CSS ::before pseudo-element

decorative_overlay_rule.trigger: position:absolute OR position:fixed + (no text content) + (no interactive children) + (pointer-events:none OR no click handlers) + (z-index < 1 OR inset:0)
decorative_overlay_rule.action: SKIP from JSON, recreate as parent::before or parent::after in companion CSS
decorative_overlay_rule.priority: Check this BEFORE applying position:fixed → HTML_WIDGET hard rule

rule_refinements: position:absolute alone does NOT trigger HTML_WIDGET (must have content/animation/JS)
display:none SKIP rule scoped to native classification only
SVG isolation: small icon container → HTML_WIDGET for icon only, not parent card
align-self → companion CSS on elementor-widget wrapper
new_companion_css_rules: CALC() values → companion CSS with px fallback in Elementor
Single-side border → companion CSS, panel border = none
aspect-ratio + object-fit on images → companion CSS
Decorative absolute overlays → parent::before in companion CSS
